summary:
Thalidomide is indeed used today as an immunomodulatory drug primarily in treating multiple myeloma and certain immune-mediated diseases like graft-versus-host disease and erythema nodosum leprosum due to its potent anti-inflammatory and immunomodulating effects. Despite being historically known for severe birth defects, it remains clinically valuable when administered with rigorous safety protocols. 

Final Answer: Yes

The summary should be concise yet fully informative regarding the core medical facts related to the query.

Thalidomide is currently used as an immunomodulatory drug in treatments including multiple myeloma and graft-versus-host disease, owing to its well-documented anti-inflammatory and immunomodal properties. Although associated with serious teratogenic risks, its therapeutic benefits justify continued use under stringent monitoring guidelines.  

Yes

This supports affirmative classification of thalidomide’s role beyond obsolete uses.
